title: Cache Write Policies and Extended Cache Concept
summary: Covers write-back and write-through cache policies, their trade-offs, and how the cache concept generalises to disk caches and browser caches.
content_points: Write-back: write only to cache; update main memory on eviction. Fast but problematic in multiprocessor systems.
Write-through: write to both cache and main memory simultaneously. Simpler coherence but slower.
Cache concept generalises: disk cache (memory as cache for disk), browser cache (disk as cache for network).
Only temporal locality applies to browser caches (no spatial locality over the network).
learning_objectives: Compare write-back and write-through cache policies
Explain why write-back requires extra consideration in multiprocessors
Describe examples of the cache concept beyond main memory
